name: Dash Rapid Egg Cooker, 6-Egg Capacity Electric Egg Cooker

specs.Capacity: 6 eggs (hard/medium/soft), 2 poached, 1 omelette, or steamed vegetables
specs.Cook Styles: Hard Boiled, Medium Boiled, Soft Boiled, Poached, Scrambled, Omelette, Steamed
specs.Time: Hard boiled in ~12 min, soft boiled in ~7 min
specs.Alert: Auto shutoff with audible done signal
specs.Water Measure: Included measuring cup with egg-piercing pin

whatWorks: The Dash Egg Cooker makes perfect hard-boiled eggs with zero attention required. Add water to the marked fill line, load eggs, press the button, and walk away. When the water evaporates the machine shuts off and beeps. Eggs come out consistently at the correct doneness every time — a task that boiling water on the stove never reliably achieves.

The egg-piercing pin on the measuring cup is a detail that matters — piercing the shell before cooking prevents cracking and produces clean, easy-to-peel hard-boiled eggs. Unpierced eggs crack in water at higher rates; this simple pin eliminates that problem. New users often skip this step until they discover its purpose.

Versatility beyond hard-boiling is genuine: the steamer tray handles poached eggs, scrambled eggs, and vegetable steaming. For households who want eggs multiple ways from the same appliance, the included tray and bowl cover all common preparations without a pot of water or separate steamer.

At $39 and approximately 6 inches tall, the Dash Egg Cooker is a storage-friendly appliance that earns counter or cabinet space by being genuinely fast and reliable. Hard-boiled eggs in 12 minutes, hands-off, with perfectly peelable results — it's the rare small appliance that does exactly one thing without compromise.
worthKnowing: Water quantity controls doneness, not time — adding more water produces harder eggs, less water produces softer eggs. The measuring cup marks three fill levels (Soft, Medium, Hard), which works well but requires reading the cup carefully. First-time users often add the wrong amount and get unexpected results.

The audible alert when cooking is complete is a single, quiet beep tone. If you're in another room or have background noise running, it's easy to miss. Some users set their own timer as a backup because the Dash alert isn't loud enough to reliably summon attention from another room.

Six-egg capacity is perfect for 1–2 people but insufficient for meal-prepping a dozen hard-boiled eggs at once. Two batches takes about 25 minutes and requires refilling water and resetting — manageable but not as efficient as a 12-egg model for batch cooking.

Mineral deposits from tap water build up in the base over time and should be descaled monthly with white vinegar. Neglecting this eventually reduces cooking efficiency and leaves a visible white coating inside the heating element. It's a 10-minute monthly maintenance task but it's necessary.

commonComplaints.title: Alert beep is too quiet to hear from another room…
commonComplaints.body: The done beep is very quiet. I've completely forgotten about my eggs twice because I was in the other room and couldn't hear it go off.

commonComplaints.title: Water amount = doneness is confusing at first…
commonComplaints.body: I didn't read the directions and added way too much water. Got rubber hard-boiled eggs. It's not hard once you understand the system but the first use was a fail.

commonComplaints.title: Only 6 eggs at a time — wish it was bigger…
commonComplaints.body: I meal prep 12 eggs every Sunday. Two batches in a row is annoying. Worth it for the consistent results but a larger capacity version would be ideal.

commonComplaints.title: Needs regular descaling or efficiency drops…
commonComplaints.body: Forgot to descale for three months. Eggs started taking longer and there was white scale all over the heating base. 10-minute vinegar soak fixed it but it's a maintenance task you can't skip.

verdict: The Dash Rapid Egg Cooker earns a 9.1 because it does its job — perfect hard-boiled eggs with zero attention — better than any other method at any price. The quiet alert and water-based doneness system have small learning curves, but once understood, this $39 appliance produces consistently peelable, perfectly done eggs faster and more reliably than stovetop boiling. For anyone who eats boiled eggs regularly, it's one of the best small-appliance purchases available per dollar.

realBuyers.quote: I've made hard-boiled eggs wrong my whole life. This machine makes them right, every single time, in 12 minutes, with no watching. Revolutionary.
The piercing pin is not optional. Pierce every egg. Since I started doing that consistently, zero cracked shells and every egg peels perfectly.
I make 6 eggs every Sunday for my week's breakfasts. Set it, forget it, done. Easiest appliance I own.
Replaced pot-boiling entirely. Less water to boil, no watching for rolling boil, no ice bath guessing, consistent results. I'll never go back.

sources.title: Dash Egg Cooker Review — Serious Eats

metaDescription: Dash Rapid Egg Cooker review for Canada: 6 eggs, hard/medium/soft boil, poach, steam, hands-off. Honest on quiet alert and monthly descaling. ~$39 CAD.
introText: The Dash Rapid Egg Cooker makes hands-off hard, medium, or soft-boiled eggs in 7–12 minutes — water quantity controls doneness, the machine auto-shuts off, and all parts are dishwasher safe. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00DDXUEF0) at around $39 CAD.
